title: quercus convallata
intro: Quercus convallata, also known as the dwarf oak or sessile oak, is a species of deciduous tree native to the mountainous regions of the Mediterranean. The bark and leaves of the Quercus convallata plant have been used in traditional medicine for their astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, Quercus convallata can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
